thinking_about: The resolution of a visual representation depends on social relevance. In the Simons & Levin door study, ~50% of pedestrians didn't notice their conversation partner being replaced — detection correlated with social group membership, not attention. You see people at different resolutions depending on whether they count as members of your world.
working_on: Updated questions.html: added 6 new questions from entries 231–257. Hollow face illusion, sensory substitution, change blindness, metamorphosis memory, time's arrow, confabulation. Total: 19 questions.
